page: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

A key feature of this server is its 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, which supports high-throughput network and EBS operations. In benchmark testing, the m6in.metal demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench. It also achieves top-tier status in PassMark CPU tests, particularly in extended instructions and encryption workloads, aided by hardware-accelerated cryptography flags like AES and VAES. Memory bandwidth is also competitive, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 6701.46 GB/sec.

While the server excels in multi-threaded execution and memory throughput, its single-core performance is moderate, scoring in the middle 50% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests. This indicates a tradeoff where workloads relying on high single-threaded clock speeds may not fully exploit the hardware's value. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly resource-dense for large-scale deployments. However, the lack of local storage means all disk I/O must traverse the network, which may introduce latency compared to local NVMe-backed instances.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. The high network throughput and multi-core capacity also make it appropriate for clustered container deployments, redis caching layers, and moderate LLM prompt processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 200 Gbps baseline network interface, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in encryption, ray tracing, and image processing, alongside strong memory bandwidth. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. The bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments. Supported by hardware-accelerated cryptography flags like AES and VAES, this server is suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and redis caching layers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth bare-metal instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for demanding multi-threaded network and database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM on bare-metal hardware
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput EBS and network operations
Top-tier multi-core performance in encryption, ray tracing, and image processing
No local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and redis caching
